notes: Solid structure with origin validation, connection+message rate limiting, input validation, and room cleanup. Security gaps: message text is NOT sanitized (only nicknames/rooms strip <>), no max payload size on WebSocket frames, no auth mechanism, no proxy-aware IP extraction. Error handling: ws.send() not wrapped in try/catch (can throw even when readyState===OPEN), no top-level try/catch in handleMessage, no error handler on HTTP server. Best practices are good: named constants, clear separation, consistent naming, but addToHistory sorts O(n log n) unnecessarily when a simple shift() would suffice. Robustness: no max rooms/clients limit (memory exhaustion vector), no heartbeat/ping-pong for dead connection detection, no graceful shutdown (SIGTERM handler, timer cleanup), no max WebSocket frame size configured.
